text.title: Data Analysis — Requirements Discovery and Quality Assessment Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real engagements applying data-analysis. Each entry pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Starting with attributes before understanding the data's purpose.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skips step 1 (identify needs) and dumps every column.
antipattern.fix: Always begin with the business question + scope; attribute set falls out from that.

antipattern.symptom: Numbers cited without their definitions become folklore.
antipattern.root-cause: Stakeholder copies a number from a Slack screenshot.
antipattern.fix: Every metric link includes a definition snippet + source-of-truth reference.

antipattern.symptom: Replacing a certified metric with an ad-hoc calculation.
antipattern.root-cause: BA writes a one-off SQL to answer a steerco question.
antipattern.fix: Reference certified metrics, never recompute; surface mismatches to analytics-engineering.

antipattern.symptom: Exploratory queries on raw PII without a DPIA.
antipattern.fix: Require synthetic/redacted env or DPIA sign-off before any direct query.
